review: Amazing food and wonderful atmosphere. The pasta was cooked to perfection and the staff was incredibly friendly.
Came here for my anniversary and it was a magical evening. The wine selection is superb and the risotto was creamy and rich.
Overrated and overpriced. The steak was tough and our waiter seemed annoyed by our questions about the menu.
Solid Italian dining experience. The tiramisu alone is worth the visit. Will definitely be coming back soon.
We waited 45 minutes past our reservation time. When we finally sat down the food was decent but not worth the hassle.
My family has been coming here for years and it never disappoints. The Sunday brunch is an absolute must-try.
The carbonara was swimming in cream which is not authentic at all. Disappointed as a fellow Italian.
Good food but the portions are small for the price. The ambiance makes up for it though. Nice date spot.
Had the chef's tasting menu and every course was a work of art. This place deserves a Michelin star.
Came with a group of 8 and they handled it perfectly. Great service and the family-style platters were generous.
Food was cold when it arrived. Sent it back and the second attempt was only marginally better. Very disappointing.
The gnocchi here is pillowy soft and the tomato sauce is clearly homemade. A hidden gem in the city.
Best sushi I've ever had outside of Japan. The chef's special omakase was worth every penny.
The fish was not fresh at all. I've had better sushi from a grocery store. Extremely overpriced for what you get.
Sat at the sushi bar and watched the chefs work. Incredible skill and the nigiri was melt-in-your-mouth good.
Good sushi but not the best in San Francisco. The dragon roll was tasty and the miso soup was comforting.
Ordered delivery and everything arrived in perfect condition. The sashimi platter was beautiful and delicious.
We had a private dining experience in the tatami room. The kaiseki meal was an unforgettable culinary journey.
The rolls are too Americanized for my taste. Too much mayo and cream cheese. I prefer traditional Japanese style.
Took my parents here for their 40th anniversary. They loved every bite. The sake selection is also top notch.
Waited an hour for our food even though the restaurant was half empty. When it came the tempura was soggy.
The lunch special is an incredible deal. Two rolls and a salad for $15. Quality is consistently great.
I found a hair in my edamame. The manager apologized and comped our appetizers but it still ruined the mood.
The yellowtail jalapeño appetizer is a must-order. Perfectly balanced heat and freshness. Addictive.
The brisket was dry and the ribs were tough. For a BBQ place in Nashville I expected much better.
Absolutely phenomenal pulled pork. Smoky and tender with just the right amount of bark. The cornbread was heavenly.
Decent BBQ but I've had better in Texas. The sauce selection is nice though and the mac and cheese was solid.
This place is a tourist trap. Long lines and mediocre food. The locals know better spots for real BBQ.
The smoked wings are out of this world. Crispy skin with a deep smoky flavor. Best wings in Nashville hands down.
Brought my buddy from Kansas City here and even he admitted the ribs were legit. That's the highest praise.
The sides are forgettable but the meats are the star. Get the three-meat combo and you won't be disappointed.
Gave me food poisoning. Spent the whole next day in bed. I suspect the potato salad was the culprit.
Live music on Friday nights makes this place even better. Great vibes and the burnt ends are incredible.
The portion sizes are enormous. We ordered for two and could have easily fed four people. Great value.
They ran out of brisket at 6pm on a Saturday. How does a BBQ place run out of brisket? Poor planning.
The peach cobbler dessert is a hidden gem on the menu. Perfectly sweet and pairs great with vanilla ice cream.
Charming French bistro with authentic cuisine. The coq au vin was divine and the crème brûlée was perfect.
Pretentious staff who looked down on us for not knowing French wine terminology. Food was good but the attitude ruined it.
The French onion soup here is the best I've ever tasted. Rich broth with perfectly caramelized onions and bubbly gruyère.
Nice ambiance but the food is overpriced for what you get. The duck confit was good but not $45 good.
Every dish was a masterpiece. The chef clearly trained in France. The bouillabaisse transported me straight to Marseille.
Went for Restaurant Week and was impressed. The prix fixe menu was an excellent value and the quality was superb.
The escargot was rubbery and the steak frites was overcooked despite ordering medium rare. Very disappointing for the price.
Beautiful restaurant with candlelit tables and soft jazz playing. Perfect for a romantic evening out. The food matches the setting.
I'm a chef myself and I can say the sauces here are technically flawless. The béarnaise was silky and balanced.
Brought my French mother-in-law here and she approved. That's basically the highest compliment a French restaurant can receive.
The cheese board was underwhelming. Only three varieties and the bread was stale. Expected more from a French bistro.
Lovely spot for Sunday brunch. The eggs Florentine and fresh croissants were delightful. Great coffee selection too.
Great street-style tacos at reasonable prices. The al pastor was my favorite. Salsa bar is a nice touch.
This is what authentic Mexican food should taste like. The carnitas are slow-cooked to perfection and the tortillas are handmade.
Meh. The tacos were bland and the guacamole tasted like it came from a jar. I know better spots in Austin.
The breakfast tacos here are legendary. Perfectly scrambled eggs with chorizo and fresh pico. I come here every Saturday morning.
Fast service and cheap prices. The quality is surprisingly good for how affordable it is. The horchata is refreshing.
Found a piece of plastic in my burrito. Showed the staff and they just shrugged. Unacceptable and unsanitary.
The fish tacos are the best in Austin. Crispy battered cod with a tangy slaw and chipotle crema. Perfection.
Good tacos but the restaurant itself is dirty. Sticky tables and floors that haven't been mopped in days.
Their queso is addictive. I literally dream about it. The chips are always fresh and warm. Simple but perfect.
Ordered the combo platter for the family and everyone was happy. Kids loved the quesadillas and I devoured the enchiladas.
The margaritas here are dangerously good. Strong and fresh with real lime juice. The food is solid too.
I'm from Mexico City and this place reminds me of the taquerias back home. Authentic flavors and generous portions.
Mediocre Chinese food. The kung pao chicken was too sweet and the fried rice was bland.
The dim sum brunch on weekends is fantastic. The har gow and siu mai are made fresh and the variety is impressive.
Ordered takeout and half the order was wrong. Called to complain and they were rude about it. Lost a customer.
The Peking duck here is the real deal. Crispy skin carved tableside with all the traditional accompaniments. A must try.
Average Chinese American food. Nothing special but nothing terrible either. Good for a quick weeknight dinner.
The hot pot experience here is wonderful. Fresh ingredients and a rich flavorful broth. Great for groups.
Way too much MSG. I had a headache for hours after eating here. The food tasted artificial and overly salty.
Hidden gem in Seattle's Chinatown. The mapo tofu is fiery and authentic. The hand-pulled noodles are mesmerizing to watch.
Brought my Chinese grandmother here and she said it was almost as good as her cooking. High praise from her.
The lunch specials are a great deal. Generous portions with soup and an egg roll included. Fast service too.
Cockroach spotted near our table. We immediately left and reported it to the health department. Absolutely disgusting.
The xiao long bao here are perfect. Thin delicate wrappers filled with savory broth and pork. Addictive little dumplings.
Wonderful vegan options that even meat lovers would enjoy. The jackfruit tacos were surprisingly delicious.
As a lifelong carnivore I was skeptical but this place converted me. The mushroom burger was better than most beef burgers I've had.
The food tastes like cardboard. Just because it's healthy doesn't mean it has to be flavorless. Very disappointed.
Love the açaí bowls here. Fresh fruit and crunchy granola with a drizzle of honey. The perfect healthy breakfast.
The smoothies are overpriced at $14 each. They're good but I can make the same thing at home for a fraction of the cost.
This place is a sanctuary for people with dietary restrictions. Gluten-free dairy-free and nut-free options clearly labeled.
The Buddha bowl was beautifully presented and tasted amazing. Quinoa roasted vegetables and a tahini dressing that was incredible.
Tried the raw vegan cheesecake and it was shockingly good. Creamy and rich without any dairy. How do they do it?
The staff is super knowledgeable about nutrition and ingredients. They helped me navigate the menu with my allergies. Much appreciated.
Portions are tiny. I left hungry and had to stop for a burger on the way home. Not satisfying at all.
The cold-pressed juices are fantastic. The green detox juice gave me so much energy. I buy one every morning now.
Cute café with a great atmosphere. Lots of plants and natural light. The avocado toast is simple but perfectly executed.
Good New York style pizza but nothing extraordinary. The garlic knots were the highlight of the meal.
The best pizza in Philly and I will fight anyone who disagrees. The margherita is simple and perfect. Fresh basil and creamy mozzarella.
Soggy crust and barely any toppings. For $18 a pizza I expected a lot more. The delivery was also an hour late.
Been coming here since I was a kid. The recipe hasn't changed in 30 years and that's exactly how it should be.
The white pizza with ricotta and spinach is heavenly. Thin crispy crust with just the right amount of garlic.
Ordered a calzone and it was basically a folded pizza with barely any filling. Not worth the extra charge.
Friday night special of a large pie and a pitcher of beer for $25 is the best deal in the neighborhood.
The meatball sub is underrated here. Everyone talks about the pizza but the subs are equally amazing. Homemade meatballs.
Rosa herself still works the counter some days. Her warmth and personality make this place feel like home.
The pizza was burnt on the bottom and raw in the middle. Sent it back and the replacement was the same. Terrible.
Great late-night spot. Open until 2am on weekends and the pizza is just as good at midnight as it is at noon.
The Sicilian slice is thick and doughy in the best way possible. Loaded with sauce and cheese. Pure comfort food.
Incredible Indian cuisine with authentic spices. The butter chicken and naan bread were outstanding.
The buffet lunch is an amazing value. Endless options and everything is freshly made. The tikka masala is my go-to.
Way too spicy even when I asked for mild. My mouth was on fire and I couldn't taste anything else. Not enjoyable.
The biryani here is layered with flavor. Fragrant basmati rice with tender lamb and the perfect blend of spices. Incredible.
Good Indian food but the restaurant is in desperate need of renovation. The carpet is stained and the lighting is harsh.
The garlic naan fresh from the tandoor is addictive. I could eat a dozen of them. Fluffy and buttery perfection.
